title: erigeron acris subsp. phaeocephalus
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. phaeocephalus, also known as the yellow daisy or alpine fleabane, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erigeron acris subsp. phaeocephalus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
